items.correct: A series of chemical reactions in cells that break down glucose to release energy as ATP.
items.misconception: Respiration means breathing — inhaling oxygen and exhaling carbon dioxide.
items.random_wrong: Respiration is the process by which plants make their own food using sunlight.
items.why: Everyday English equates 'respiration' with breathing; students rarely separate gas exchange from cellular metabolism.

items.correct: Yes — plants respire all the time to release energy; they also photosynthesise when there is light.
items.misconception: Plants only photosynthesise and do not respire; respiration is for animals only.
items.random_wrong: Plants only respire at night and only photosynthesise during the day; the processes never overlap.
items.why: Because photosynthesis is taught as the plant equivalent of respiration, students think it replaces respiration in plants entirely.

items.q: What is produced in anaerobic respiration in human muscle cells?
items.correct: Lactic acid (lactate) and a small amount of ATP — no CO2 or water in this pathway.
items.misconception: Anaerobic respiration produces ethanol and CO2 in humans.
items.random_wrong: Anaerobic respiration produces oxygen as a waste product.
items.why: Students confuse anaerobic respiration in yeast (alcohol + CO2) with the human form (lactic acid).
It's alveoli. “trachea” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: contracts and flattens. “relaxes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Lungs have about 500 million alveoli. Total surface area is about 70 m². Estimate area per alveolus in 10⁻⁷ m².
items.why: Exact answer: 1.4 x10^-7 m^2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
